title: Radians & degrees
description: Convert angle measures given in degrees to radians and vice versa.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Convert the angle $\theta=\dfrac{8\pi}{9}$ radians to degrees.**  
*Express your answer exactly.*

$\theta=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] $^\circ$
hints:
###Background 

An angle can be measured in degrees or in radians. A circle can be divided into $360^\circ$ or $2\pi$ radians. The conversion between degrees and radians is as follows. 

$\text{Angle in Radians} = \dfrac{\pi}{180^\circ}\cdot\text{Angle in Degrees}$ 

$\text{Angle in Degrees} = \dfrac{180^\circ}{\pi}\cdot\text{Angle in Radians}$

###Summary 

$\theta=160^\circ$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Convert the angle $\theta=260^\circ$ to radians.**  
*Express your answer exactly.*

$\theta=$ [[☃ expression 1]] radians
hints:
###Background 

An angle can be measured in degrees or in radians. A circle can be divided into $360^\circ$ or $2\pi$ radians. The conversion between degrees and radians is as follows. 

$\text{Angle in Radians} = \dfrac{\pi}{180^\circ}\cdot\text{Angle in Degrees}$ 

$\text{Angle in Degrees} = \dfrac{180^\circ}{\pi}\cdot\text{Angle in Radians}$

###Converting $\theta$ to radians 

Using the formula, we get the following conversion. 

$\begin{align}\text{Angle in Radians} &= \dfrac{\pi}{180^\circ}\cdot\text{Angle in Degrees}
\\\\ &= \dfrac{\pi}{180^\circ}\cdot260^\circ
\\\\&=\dfrac{13\pi}{9}\end{align}$

###Summary 

$\theta=\dfrac{13\pi}{9}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Convert the angle $\theta=180^\circ$ to radians.**  
*Express your answer exactly.*

$\theta=$ [[☃ expression 1]] radians
hints:
###Background 

An angle can be measured in degrees or in radians. A circle can be divided into $360^\circ$ or $2\pi$ radians. The conversion between degrees and radians is as follows. 

$\text{Angle in Radians} = \dfrac{\pi}{180^\circ}\cdot\text{Angle in Degrees}$ 

$\text{Angle in Degrees} = \dfrac{180^\circ}{\pi}\cdot\text{Angle in Radians}$

###Converting $\theta$ to radians 

Using the formula, we get the following conversion. 

$\begin{align}\text{Angle in Radians} &= \dfrac{\pi}{180^\circ}\cdot\text{Angle in Degrees}
\\\\ &= \dfrac{\pi}{180^\circ}\cdot180^\circ
\\\\&=\pi\end{align}$

###Summary 

$\theta=\pi$
